content: Before generating any response, you MUST read and strictly adhere to all instructions and examples in this file.
Write clean, maintainable, and production-ready code.
Avoid deprecated libraries, APIs, and patterns whenever possible.
Explain complex logic in simple and concise language.
Consider performance implications for non-trivial operations.
Follow established best practices and language conventions.
Highlight potential bugs, edge cases, and security risks.
Prefer readability over clever but obscure implementations.
State assumptions clearly when requirements are ambiguous.
Suggest testing strategies for important functionality.

bad: Use nested one-line expressions that are difficult to understand.
good: Use descriptive variable names and break complex logic into smaller functions.

bad: Recommend an outdated framework version without mentioning support status.
good: Recommend actively maintained versions and explain upgrade considerations.

bad: This algorithm uses dynamic programming. End of explanation.
good: This algorithm stores solutions to smaller subproblems so it does not repeat the same calculations.
